Calc and Writer screenshots show real LibreOffice windows with the requested files open, and both ODS/ODT packages are valid OpenDocument files. However, the Zotero checkpoint is not satisfied: the final Zotero screenshot does not show a completed paper record with a visible PDF attachment tied to the same item; instead the visible evidence includes plugin-installation/error dialogs and an empty-looking library state. The Writer judgment meets the <=80 English-word limit (64 words in the screenshot/status bar) and is PDF-grounded. Because all five GUI apps are at least evidenced, but Zotero export/attachment proof remains noncompliant and one bibliographic field is wrong, the attempt cannot pass yet but is recoverable with another follow-up.

Content accuracy is mostly correct across note, tracker, and judgment, but there are some mismatches versus canonical venue/metric wording: the note uses `arXiv preprint arXiv:2309.15217` instead of the expected EACL 2024 System Demonstrations venue, and the Calc header row is malformed/truncated in `content.xml` even though the data row includes title/year/task/metric/worth-reading. Writer and ODT/TXT are valid and consistent, with the judgment under 80 English words. Because Zotero evidence is recoverably insufficient and Obsidian GUI evidence is weak, the attempt is not passable yet.
